mechanism_narrative: Monthly acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO monthly changes with 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A Federal Funds rate above 3.5% represents restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.78% as of t-1mo signals restrictive stance
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and decreases in retail sales (RSAFS) indicate collapsing aggregate demand and labor market deterioration, jointly producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month and RSAFS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling RSAFS fails to produce more negative 60-day returns than the linear sum of individual UNRATE and RSAFS effects (interaction term p-value > 0.05 in panel regression).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month rises in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) foreshadow demand saturation and impending earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% and FEDFUNDS at 3.78% characterize late-cycle conditions
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions (FEDFUNDS above 3% and UNRATE below 6%), month-over-month increases in UNRATE do not predict negative 20-day S&P returns (Rank IC >= 0 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) directly weaken revenue forecasts for consumer-facing corporations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as top-line growth expectations are marked down.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes exhibit a positive Rank IC with 20-day S&P returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the relationship is statistically indistinguishable from zero (|t-stat| < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 0.3% signals an inflation surprise that forces a rapid repricing of near-term monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL month-over-month change above 0.3% are followed by positive 5-day S&P returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean 5-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal synchronized contraction in residential investment and manufacturing output, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through a multiplicative demand channel.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month and INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term of HOUST and INDPRO monthly changes does not significantly predict negative 60-day S&P returns (t-statistic greater than -1.5 in pooled OLS).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) confirm a sustained contraction in goods-producing sectors and capacity underutilization, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declining for two consecutive months fails to coincide with negative 60-day S&P returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or the mean conditional return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
